title: papaver dubium var. vagum
intro: Papaver dubium var. vagum, also known as the yellow poppy or lesser meadow poppy, is a perennial plant species within the Papaver genus, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and delicate foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Papaver dubium var. vagum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to support respiratory health and immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve drying and infusing the flowers or leaves in hot water to create a tea, or using the roots and leaves in a tincture or ointment to topical applications.
